costs.question: Is OpenHospi really free?
costs.answer: Yes, OpenHospi is completely free for all students. There are no hidden fees, no premium plans, and no ads. We are funded by donations and sponsorships.

costs.question: How is OpenHospi funded?
costs.answer: OpenHospi is currently self-funded by its founder. We are actively looking for donors and sponsors to help cover infrastructure costs. All contributions will be managed transparently via Open Collective.

costs.question: Will OpenHospi ever become paid?
costs.answer: No. OpenHospi will never be freemium or pay-to-win. No student will ever have to pay to use the platform or get more visibility.

safety.question: How are users verified on OpenHospi?
safety.answer: Every user logs in through their own university or hogeschool. This means only real students of Dutch educational institutions can use the platform.

safety.question: Are my messages private?
safety.answer: Yes. All chat messages on OpenHospi are end-to-end encrypted. Not even OpenHospi can read your conversations.

safety.question: What happens when I report a user?
safety.answer: Reports are reviewed by our team. We take action quickly, including blocking users who violate our terms of service.

e2eEncryption.question: Can OpenHospi read my messages?
e2eEncryption.answer: No. Messages are encrypted on your device before being sent. We never have access to your decryption keys, so we cannot read your messages.

e2eEncryption.question: What happens if I lose my device?
e2eEncryption.answer: You can recover your identity keys using your PIN backup. Without the PIN, old messages cannot be decrypted. This is a security feature, not a bug.

e2eEncryption.question: What protocol does OpenHospi use?
e2eEncryption.answer: We use a Signal Protocol-inspired architecture with X3DH key exchange and Double Ratchet for per-message forward secrecy.

e2eEncryption.question: Can I verify someone's identity?
e2eEncryption.answer: Yes. You can compare safety numbers with each member individually in the conversation info screen. If they match, no one has intercepted that connection.

howItWorks.question: How do I find a room on OpenHospi?
howItWorks.answer: Log in with your institutional account, complete your profile, browse available rooms by city and price, and send a personal message to houses you like.

howItWorks.question: What is hospiteren?
howItWorks.answer: Hospiteren is the traditional Dutch way of finding housemates. Houses invite candidates for a visit so both sides can see if it's a good fit before moving in.

about.title: About OpenHospi
about.subtitle: A free, open-source platform to find and list student rooms in the Netherlands, built by students, for students.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: OpenHospi exists to make finding student housing in the Netherlands fair, free, and accessible. We believe finding a room shouldn't cost money, and the process should be transparent. OpenHospi will never be freemium or pay-to-win: no one can pay to get more visibility. Every student gets the same chances.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: A world where every student in the Netherlands has equal access to housing. No paid platforms, no gatekeepers, just a fair, transparent process where your personality matters more than your wallet.

problem.title: Why OpenHospi?
problem.description1: Students today search for rooms through Facebook groups, Instagram, and paid platforms. On commercial sites, you pay to see listings or get priority placement. The system works for platforms, not for students.
problem.description2: We believe this is fundamentally unfair. Housing is a basic need, and students shouldn't be charged to find a place to live. That's why we built OpenHospi: a platform where everyone gets the same visibility, the same tools, and the same chances, completely free.

members.name: Ruben Talstra
members.bio: BITM student at Hanze University of Applied Sciences and self-taught software engineer with over 10 years of experience. Started OpenHospi to give every student a fair chance at finding housing. No fees, no favoritism.

openSource.title: Open Source & Community
openSource.description: OpenHospi is fully open source. Anyone can inspect the code, report issues, suggest features, or contribute directly. Transparency isn't a feature, it's a principle. We believe that a platform for students should be owned by the community.

costs.title: Costs & Transparency
costs.subtitle: OpenHospi is free for all users. Here's what it costs us to run.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about OpenHospi: a free, open-source platform to find and list student rooms.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.description: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Reach out to us directly.

items.question: What is OpenHospi?
items.answer: OpenHospi is a free, open-source platform where students in the Netherlands can find and list rooms. It connects students looking for a room with houses looking for a new housemate, through the traditional Dutch hospiteren process.

items.question: Who can use OpenHospi?
items.answer: Only verified students at Dutch universities and hogescholen. You log in with your institutional account, so every user is a real student.

items.question: Is OpenHospi available outside the Netherlands?
items.answer: Currently, OpenHospi is only available for students at Dutch educational institutions with institutional login. We may expand in the future.

items.question: Is OpenHospi affiliated with any university or company?
items.answer: No. OpenHospi is an independent, community-driven open-source project. It was started by a student and is not owned or operated by any university, company, or housing corporation.

items.question: How do I create an account?
items.answer: Click 'Log in with your institution' and sign in with your university or hogeschool account. Your account is created automatically. No separate registration needed.

items.question: Why can I only log in with my institution?
items.answer: Institutional verification ensures every user is a real, verified student. This prevents fake profiles, scams, and non-students from using the platform.

items.question: What happens when I graduate?
items.answer: When your institution deactivates your account, you'll no longer be able to log in. Any active room listings will be automatically paused.

items.question: Can I delete my account?
items.answer: Yes. You can delete your account and all associated data from your profile settings at any time. This action is permanent and cannot be undone.

items.question: How do I apply for a room?
items.answer: Browse available rooms, find one you like, and send a personal message to the house explaining why you'd be a good fit. If they're interested, they'll invite you for hospiteren.

items.question: What is hospiteren?
items.answer: Hospiteren is the traditional Dutch way of finding housemates. The house invites candidates for a visit: you meet the current housemates, see the room, and both sides decide if it's a good match.

items.question: Can I filter rooms by city, price, or other criteria?
items.answer: Yes. You can filter rooms by city, price range, room size, availability date, and house type to find exactly what you're looking for.

items.question: Do all students get the same visibility?
items.answer: Yes. There are no premium placements or paid boosts. Every student's application is treated equally. No one can pay to get ahead.

items.question: How do I list a room?
items.answer: Log in, create your house profile with photos and details, then publish your room listing. Verified students across the Netherlands can see it immediately.

items.question: Can I share my listing on social media?
items.answer: Yes. Every room gets a unique public link you can share on Instagram, WhatsApp, or anywhere else. Anyone can view the listing, but only verified students can apply.

items.question: How many rooms can I list?
items.answer: You can list one room per house at a time. If you have multiple rooms available, you can create separate listings for each.

items.question: Can I pause or remove my listing?
items.answer: Yes. You can pause your listing to temporarily hide it, or remove it entirely when the room is filled. You're always in control.

items.question: Are my messages encrypted?
items.answer: Yes. All chat messages on OpenHospi are end-to-end encrypted (E2EE), including group conversations. Not even OpenHospi can read your messages, only you and the other members of your conversation.

items.question: Does OpenHospi use trackers or sell my data?
items.answer: No. We don't use third-party trackers, analytics cookies, or sell your data to anyone. Your privacy is fundamental to how we operate.

items.question: How do I report inappropriate behavior?
items.answer: You can report or block any user directly from their profile or chat. Reports are reviewed by our team and we act quickly. You can also email abuse@openhospi.nl.

items.question: How does OpenHospi protect against scams?
items.answer: Every user is verified by their institution, so you always know you're dealing with a real student. Combined with E2E encrypted chat and easy reporting, we make it very hard for bad actors.

items.question: Is OpenHospi really free?
items.answer: Yes, 100% free. No hidden fees, no premium plans, no ads. OpenHospi will never be freemium or pay-to-win.

items.question: How is the platform funded?
items.answer: OpenHospi is currently self-funded by its founder. We are actively looking for donors and sponsors to help cover infrastructure costs. All contributions will be managed transparently via Open Collective.

items.question: Will there ever be paid features?
items.answer: No. We believe finding student housing should be fair and equal. No student will ever have to pay to use the platform, get more visibility, or access features that others can't.

e2eEncryption.title: How End-to-End Encryption Works
e2eEncryption.subtitle: Complete transparency about how OpenHospi protects every conversation between seekers and house members.

overview.title: What is End-to-End Encryption?
overview.description: On OpenHospi, every conversation is between a room seeker and the house members. All messages are end-to-end encrypted (E2EE): they are encrypted on your device before being sent and can only be decrypted by the other members of your conversation. Not even OpenHospi can read them.

pairwiseEncryption.title: Pairwise Encryption
pairwiseEncryption.description: OpenHospi uses pairwise encryption, the same approach used by Signal and WhatsApp. When you send a message, your device encrypts it separately for each member in the conversation using their individual encryption session. The server stores one encrypted copy per recipient and can never see the actual message. Each member can only decrypt the copy that was encrypted specifically for them.

perRecipient.label: Per-recipient encryption
perRecipient.detail: Each message is encrypted separately for every member

independentSessions.label: Independent sessions
independentSessions.detail: Every pair of members has its own encryption session

noSharedKey.label: No shared key
noSharedKey.detail: Compromising one session cannot affect others

identityKeys.title: Identity Keys
identityKeys.description: Every user gets a unique cryptographic identity based on Ed25519 and X25519 keys. These keys are generated on your device and never leave it. Your public identity key is shared with other conversation members so they can verify who they're talking to.
identityKeys.ed25519: Digital signatures for identity verification and key authentication.
identityKeys.x25519: Elliptic-curve Diffie-Hellman for secure key agreement between members.

keyExchange.title: Key Exchange (X3DH)
keyExchange.description: When a conversation starts, the Extended Triple Diffie-Hellman (X3DH) protocol establishes a shared secret between each pair of members, even if some are offline. For a conversation with three members, three separate X3DH exchanges take place (one for each pair). Pre-uploaded keys on the server make this possible without ever revealing private keys.

doubleRatchet.title: Double Ratchet
doubleRatchet.description: After X3DH, every pair of members maintains its own Double Ratchet session. Each message uses a unique encryption key that is immediately discarded after use. Keys rotate with every message, providing forward secrecy: even if a key is compromised, past messages remain safe.

messageEncryption.title: Message Encryption
messageEncryption.description: Each copy of a message is encrypted with AES-256-GCM, an industry-standard authenticated encryption algorithm. A unique key and initialization vector (IV) are generated per recipient, per message. The message header is included as additional authenticated data (AAD) to prevent tampering.

safetyNumbers.title: Safety Numbers
safetyNumbers.description: You can verify each member individually by comparing safety numbers in the conversation info screen. These 60-digit codes are derived from both users' identity keys and are unique to each pair, so you can confirm that no one is impersonating a house member or seeker.

serverComparison.title: What the Server Sees
serverComparison.stored: Encrypted ciphertexts (one per recipient, per message), message metadata (timestamps, conversation IDs), and public keys. None of this reveals message content.
serverComparison.never: Your actual messages, files, or any plaintext content. Decryption keys never touch our servers. The server only relays encrypted data it cannot read.

openSource.title: Open Source & Auditable
openSource.description: All encryption code is open source. You can review, audit, or contribute to our cryptographic implementations on GitHub.

alternatives.title: Alternatives & Comparison
alternatives.subtitle: An honest look at the Dutch student housing landscape. We believe in transparency, including about our competitors.
alternatives.disclaimer: Platforms change. Last updated: March 2026. See something wrong? Email us at info@openhospi.nl.

items.name: OpenHospi
items.pricing: Free: no premium, no ads, no paywalls
strengths: Every user verified via institutional login (university-backed)
Fully open source: anyone can audit the code
Mobile app for iOS and Android
weaknesses: New platform: still growing user base and city coverage
items.bestFor: Students who want a free, fair, and private housing platform with verified users.

items.name: Hospi.nl
strengths: Strong house-side workflow (star voting, private notes, roles)
Shared inbox and house chat features
Established platform in the Dutch hospi scene
items.bestFor: Houses looking for a powerful hospi management tool with roles and permissions.

items.name: myHospi.nl
items.pricing: Free core. Boost €1.99, Premium week €3.99, month €8.99, 3 months €19.99, Super reactions €0.99–€4.99
strengths: Rich seeker profiles with video support
Candidate overview with notes and one-click invites
weaknesses: Pay-to-win: boosts, premium, and super reactions give paying users advantages
items.bestFor: Seekers who want rich profile features and don't mind paying for extra visibility.

items.name: Dingdong
weaknesses: Premium+ gives paying users more visibility
Less traditional hospi management compared to Hospi.nl
items.bestFor: Seekers who prefer a swipe/match-oriented experience at a low cost.

items.name: Kamernet
items.pricing: Seekers: €38/4 weeks, €75/12 weeks, €98/6 months. Listing is free.
strengths: Huge reach: the largest Dutch room marketplace
Shared inbox and house voting features
weaknesses: Seekers must pay to message: €38+ barrier to entry
items.bestFor: Anyone (not just students) who wants maximum reach and is willing to pay for access.

items.name: Facebook Groups
strengths: Easy to share listings in multiple groups
weaknesses: No structured hospi workflow at all
No user verification: scams are common
No dedicated housing tools or filters
items.bestFor: Quick, informal room searches where reach matters more than structure.

honesty.title: Why we're honest about this
honesty.description: We built OpenHospi because we believe student housing should be free and fair. But being fair also means being honest about the landscape. Some platforms have features we don't have yet. Some have more users. We're not here to trash competitors. We're here to give you the full picture so you can make the best choice for your situation. If another platform works better for you right now, use it. We'll be here when you want something free, private, and built for students.

cta.title: Want to try OpenHospi?
cta.subtitle: Free, fair, and verified. No strings attached.
